title: Neonauclea cyclophylla
intro: Neonauclea cyclophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "blood tree" due to its deep red sap. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections. Bioactive constituents of Neonauclea cyclophylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Neonauclea cyclophylla is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Within the family Rubiaceae, Neonauclea cyclophylla is further classified into the genus Neonauclea. The genus Neonauclea belongs to the tribe Naucleae. The species Neonauclea cyclophylla is native to the Philippines.
uses: Neonauclea cyclophylla has been found to have potential medicinal uses, particularly in the treatment of various health issues due to its rich content of bioactive compounds. Its leaves and roots have been traditionally used in folk medicine to address symptoms of fever, cough, and cold. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. Research suggests that Neonauclea cyclophylla may also have antioxidant and antiviral properties, making it a promising candidate for further investigation. Further studies are needed to fully explore the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Neonauclea cyclophylla.
